extra.reason: Superseded by data-vb-event analytics attribute system. The 'data-track' proposal was reframed to 'data-vb-event' to match the canonical vb:* namespace (see <meta name="vb:*"> taxonomy). Implementation: src/lib/analytics.js handleDocumentClick + extractDeclarativeProps. Docs: site/src/pages/docs/attributes/data-vb-event.html. Properties via data-vb-event-* dataset → camelCase props. Auto-wired by the analytics runtime on boot.
Shipped: reading-progress component with progressive enhancement (CSS scroll-timeline + JS fallback). Doc page + demo + future-wc updated.

Superseded: avatar-group functionality already covered by <user-avatar> + <layout-cluster data-layout-overlap>. See site/src/pages/docs/elements/custom-elements/user-avatar.html (Avatar Group section). Adding a wrapper component would violate IA rule #2 (enhancement is a layout behavior on existing elements, not a new component).

Shipped: breakpoint-specimen with viewport + container (data-observe) modes. Pure-CSS active highlight via @media for viewport mode; ResizeObserver for container mode. Doc page + demo + sidebar entry + future-wc updated.

Shipped: data-weight, data-rollup (sum|weighted-sum|avg|max), data-heatmap (auto|low-good|high-good) on <th>. Decision-matrix is now a recipe documented on the data-table doc page. Verified math + sort + heatmap in browser.

Shipped: <quadrant-grid> generic 2x2 with x/y axis labels, q1..q4 quadrant titles (Cartesian), draggable opt-in DnD via drag-surface, data-x/data-y coordinate pinning. Verified basic + SWOT + stakeholder-map demos in browser; quadrant-grid:move event fires with correct from/to indices. Doc page includes both recipes.

Shipped: <risk-register> composes <data-table> + <quadrant-grid> from one <template> source. Severity computed via product rollup; low-good heatmap. Side-by-side at >=64rem, stacked below. Verified math (4*3=12, 2*5=10, 5*3=15) and dot positioning in browser.

Shipped: retrospective-board recipe doc section added to kanban-board doc page + standalone demo at demos/examples/demos/kanban-retrospective.html. No new component required — kanban-board already supports arbitrary <section data-column> children with data-column-color.
